title: HTML Entities Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential codes for displaying special characters in HTML.

questions.question: Which entity should you use to display a less-than symbol (<) that is not part of a tag?
What is the result of using &nbsp; in HTML?
options: It adds a new line to the paragraph.
It adds a non-breaking space that prevents the browser from splitting two words into different lines.
It formats the following text as bold.
It inserts a horizontal line (rule) on the page.

questions.question: Which of these entities represents the copyright symbol (©)?
Every HTML entity must begin and end with which two characters?
Why can't you simply type more than one space in a row in a standard HTML paragraph?
options: HTML code has a 100-character limit per line.
Browsers normally collapse multiple spaces into a single space.
Only specialized fonts support multiple spaces.
Spaces are reserved for tag attributes.
